title: helleborus corsicus subsp. lividus
intro: Helleborus corsicus subsp. lividus, also known as the Corsican hellebore, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the Helleborus genus. It is a perennial plant with dark green, leathery leaves and nodding, bell-shaped flowers in shades of green and white. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its emetic, purgative, and cathartic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Helleborus corsicus subsp. lividus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, but it should be used with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
